exercises.title: DECRYPT
exercises.brief: Parse a JSON packet and read its score.
promptLines: A sensor sends its reading as a JSON **string** like `'{"score":42}'`. Define **parseScore(jsonStr)** that parses the string with `JSON.parse` and returns the value of the `score` field.
starterLines: // TODO: JSON.parse the string, return its score field
exercises.lore: Crack the packet's seal and the score bleeds out in plaintext.

exercises.title: TALLY CORE
exercises.brief: Reduce a column of records to one total.
promptLines: `records` is an array of objects. Define **sumField(records, key)** that returns the sum of the numeric value at `key` across every record. An empty array totals `0`.
starterLines: // TODO: reduce the records, summing each record[key]
solutionLines: return records.reduce(function (acc, r) { return acc + r[key]; }, 0);
exercises.lore: The tally core melts a whole ledger column into a single glowing number.

exercises.title: MEAN FILTER
exercises.brief: Average a field across all records.
promptLines: `records` is an array of objects. Define **averageField(records, key)** that returns the mean (average) of the value at `key`. Sum the field, then divide by the record count. An empty array returns `0` (never divide by zero).
starterLines: // TODO: return the mean of record[key]; empty -> 0
solutionLines: const total = records.reduce(function (acc, r) { return acc + r[key]; }, 0);
exercises.lore: The mean filter smooths a thousand noisy pings into one steady reading.

exercises.title: CENSUS
promptLines: `records` is an array of objects. Define **countByField(records, key)** returning an object mapping each distinct value at `key` to the count of records having it. An empty array returns `{}`.
solutionLines: for (const r of records) { const v = r[key]; out[v] = (out[v] || 0) + 1; }
exercises.lore: Headcount the datastream, faction by faction.

exercises.title: APEX SCAN
exercises.brief: Find the record topping a field.
promptLines: `records` is an array of objects. Define **maxByField(records, key)** that returns the whole record whose value at `key` is the largest. On an empty array, return `null`.
starterLines: // TODO: return the record with the largest record[key]; empty -> null
solutionLines: for (const r of records) { if (r[key] > best[key]) best = r; }
exercises.hint: Guard empty -> null, seed best = records[0], loop keeping the larger record[key].
exercises.lore: The apex scan locks onto the strongest signal in the swarm and ignores the rest.
